body.title: Librarian tools: unify 4 if-chain dispatchers to handler-dict pattern
body.result: handler-dict dispatch in 4 files; 70 lines removed; 214 tests pass
body.intention: I intend that Librarian tool dispatch uses a consistent handler-dict pattern instead of per-file if/elif chains, making it trivial to add new tools without hunting dispatch locations.
body.description:
igor_tools.py:dispatch (8 branches), palace_tools.py:dispatch (4), budget_tools.py:dispatch (5), channel_tools.py:dispatch (5) all use the same if/elif-on-name pattern. Replace with a _HANDLERS = {name: fn} dict per file and a single dispatch call.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/librarian/tools/igor_tools.py, palace_tools.py, budget_tools.py, channel_tools.py
**Design rules:** OOP-first; no-sqlite
**Scope boundary:** Replace if/elif chains in dispatch() with dict lookup. No behavior changes. No new tools.
**Completion criteria:** All 4 dispatch() methods use dict lookup; existing tests pass; adding a new tool requires adding one dict entry only.
